description: Top-level card-sorting rules for non-destructive repo cleanup and cloud replication.

rules.name: keep-source-in-git
rules.reason: Active code, tests, deployment assets, or maintained documentation.

rules.name: repo-metadata-in-git
rules.reason: Core repo metadata and build configuration.

rules.name: safe-examples-in-git
rules.reason: Template environment files are safe to keep in source control.

rules.name: datasets-and-training-manual-review
rules.reason: Mixed corpora, runs, notebooks, and training assets should be curated before publishing.

rules.name: notes-and-content-manual-review
rules.reason: Human-authored knowledge may belong in Git, cloud archive, or both depending on sensitivity.

rules.name: generated-outputs-to-gdrive
rules.reason: Generated outputs should be replicated to cloud storage instead of bloating the repo.

rules.name: cache-and-runtime-ignore
rules.reason: Runtime caches and local dependency trees should not be exported or pushed.

rules.name: archival-snapshots-to-gdrive
rules.reason: Snapshots, mirrors, and backups are better treated as archives than live source.

rules.name: ops-state-manual-review
rules.reason: Agent and local orchestration state may be useful to preserve, but should not be moved blindly.

rules.name: secret-files-security-review
rules.reason: Sensitive local configuration and databases need explicit handling before any export.
